title: Chengdu
html:
Chengdu

The capital of Sichuan and the second pole-city of Yahir's China chapter — visited from his Shanghai base in late September 2024, captured most thoroughly in 2024 09 25 things to do in chengdu as a sights-plus-meal-cost log. Chengdu matters in the corpus less for what Yahir saw and more for what the note structure reveals about his mind: it's the first instance of his China chapter trip-planning template, which he then reused intact for Xi'an in November (2024 11 07 xi an). Two trips, same scaffold — sights / food / nightlife, broken out by day, with a parallel meal-by-meal cost log appended.

The Chengdu sights list reads like a Sichuan greatest-hits set: Mount Qingcheng (Taoist mountain), Sichuan opera at the Shufeng Yayun Teahouse / Jinjiang Theater, Qing Yang Palace (Taoism temple fair), Jin Li ancient street festival (lantern displays), and the Chunxi Road / Jinjiang River area for general urban roaming. The Sichuan food line in the note carries the bracketed emphasis "time to grub" — Yahir genuinely came for the food.

The cost log is the more revealing artifact. Thirteen line items totaling 362 CNY ($50 USD) over the trip: 1st meal (72), 2nd (16), pastries (5), Mixue ice cream cone (2), 3rd meal (34), Magnum bar (10), 4th meal (68), 5th frog meal (97), Blizzard (28), water (5), Mixue pineapple (8), pre-panda breakfast (8.5). The "5th frog meal" at 97 CNY is the standout — Sichuan-style frog is a Chengdu specialty and the priciest item on his trip. The "pre-panda breakfast" line is the only confirmed itinerary detail beyond the bucket list — he visited a panda research base. The fact that he tracked every meal cost in CNY is itself the point: this is the same founder-as-P&L instinct that hardens into no runway discipline in 2025 09 23 how im scraping by a year later. Yahir has always treated travel as a budgeted operation.

The travel stories catalog is curiously silent on Chengdu specifically — no mishap entry made the bank — which suggests the trip went smoothly enough to be the control case against which messier trips (India hospital, Egypt food poisoning) are remembered. Chengdu was the food-and-temples version of China; Xi'an was the imperial-history version.
